element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Fundamental attack and jump mechanics established immediately
Health restoration and bonus items hidden within, teaching resource discovery
Enemies recur throughout all stages; players learn patterns early
Activated by pressing Attack+Jump; enables crowd control, differentiates character playstyles
Found in breakable objects; sword is Carlos's special move resource
One boss per stage, escalating difficulty through game progression
Temporary invulnerability relief mechanic; rare occurrence
Hidden resource, incentivizes thorough environment exploration
Overarching Mad Gear plot unfolds across six geographically distinct stages
Visual and thematic stage differentiation (Hong Kong → France → Holland → England → Italy → Japan) maintains player interest

typical_relief_triggers: Finding health-restoring food in breakable objects
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based tension escalation with boss climaxes at each stage end; relief provided by stage completion and environmental resource discovery; two-player cooperative mode distributes pressure dynamically

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Destructible drums and barrels contain hidden items; encourages environmental interaction and thorough exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Health items and bonus collectibles concealed within interactive environment objects; requires breaking/interaction to discover

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 (Hong Kong) establishes core mechanics with Won Won boss; manageable enemy density and attack patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 2-4 (France, Holland, England) introduce repeated enemy types with varied boss patterns; difficulty steady-state with incremental complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 5-6 (Italy, Japan) feature Rolento (returning arcade boss) and Retu (final leader); highest enemy density and boss complexity concentrated here
notable_spikes: Rolento boss appearance (Stage 5) - returning arcade character with advanced moveset
Stage 6 (Japan) final stage concentration of mechanics mastery required
Expert difficulty mode unlocks additional ending content, signaling true completion challenge

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of six stages follows: hordes of underlings → escalating enemy pressure → boss encounter → stage completion/relief. Bonus stages (car break, drum break) provide interstitial relief between boss fights.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage 1 Hong Kong: Won Won boss; Stage 2 France: Freddie boss + bonus car break stage

notable_level_patterns.description: Six stages set in distinct Eurasian locations (Hong Kong, France, Holland, England, Italy, Japan) creating thematic variety and narrative forward momentum without gameplay deviation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Progression from urban Asia (Hong Kong) through European locations (France, Holland, England, Italy) to return origin (Japan)

notable_level_patterns.description: Health items and weapons hidden in breakable drums/barrels/objects, requiring player to interact with environment and manage resource discovery timing
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All stages feature scattered breakable objects; bonus stages dedicated to environment destruction (Break Car, Break Drum)

notable_level_patterns.description: Three playable characters (Haggar-wrestling, Maki-ninjutsu, Carlos-sword-martial arts) require different tactical approaches; encourages replay and character specialization
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Carlos's sword-based special move creates distinct positioning requirements vs. Haggar's grappling or Maki's ninja techniques
